title: Request Targets, Host Authority, Client Identity, and Proxy Trust · Concurrent Code Workshop
summary: A server pairing session predicts request, handler, dependency, response, and cleanup transitions before building the first request authority and trusted-proxy decision record for request targets, host authority, client identity, and proxy trust. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of request authority and trusted-proxy decision record while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and e…
objectives: Interpret Method, URL, Host, RequestURI, Header, Body, Trailer, RemoteAddr, TLS, Pattern, and Context using server-side semantics.
Validate authoritative host and port against a configured allowlist before routing, link construction, cache keys, or tenant selection.
Accept Forwarded or X-Forwarded values only from configured proxy hops and derive client, scheme, and host with a documented traversal rule.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Go server Commit to the mission model · Workshop Server Request Field Meaning
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop team handles case 5625 in a partner API with an outdated generated client. Build deterministic Go evidence to derive effective authority only through an explicit trusted-proxy boundary; then replay the same operation key with different content and reject the mismatch. Browser code models decisions only. Listener, TLS, database, proxy, race, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Go server Commit to the mission model · Workshop Server Request Field Meaning.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for interpret method, url, host, requesturi, header, body, trailer, remoteaddr, tls, pattern, and context using server-side semantics. For Go server Commit to the mission model · Workshop Server Request Field Meaning, keep Server Request Field Meaning connected to executable HTTP client decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Record low-cardinality route, request ID, trusted peer, and redacted target evidence without logging credentials, tokens, or personal data.

hints: For Go server Defend the transferred model · Workshop Request Identity Redaction, name the method, authority, body, byte, time, and credential boundary, Request Identity Redaction precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Go server Defend the transferred model · Workshop Request Identity Redaction, trace this concrete input and predict resolution, redirect, status, representation, retry, and cleanup evidence before reviewing it: A workshop team handles case 5634 in a canary service revision draining long-lived streams. Build deterministic Go evidence to derive effective authority only through an explicit trusted-proxy boundary; then return a five-hundred response and retain a low-cardinality causal trace. Browser code models decisions only. Listener, TLS, database, proxy, race, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.
